articles.title: Is Smurfit Westrock’s (SW) NYSE-Only Listing a Signal About Its Long-Term Investor Base Strategy?
articles.summary: Smurfit Westrock Plc is delisting its ordinary shares from the London Stock Exchange by June 19, 2026, to consolidate its listing on the New York Stock Exchange. This decision was driven by low LSE trading volumes and high regulatory costs, aiming to streamline investor access and potentially enhance the company's long-term appeal. The move occurs amidst ongoing restructuring, plant closures, and efforts to integrate operations while targeting US$400 million in synergies.
articles.content: Smurfit Westrock Plc is delisting its ordinary shares from the London Stock Exchange by June 19, 2026, to consolidate its listing on the New York Stock Exchange. This decision was driven by low LSE trading volumes and high regulatory costs, aiming to streamline investor access and potentially enhance the company's long-term appeal. The move occurs amidst ongoing restructuring, plant closures, and efforts to integrate operations while targeting US$400 million in synergies.

articles.title: Industrial-Led Beat And Higher Guidance Could Be A Game Changer For Analog Devices (ADI)
articles.summary: Analog Devices reported strong second-quarter results with sales of US$3.62 billion and net income of US$1.18 billion, driven by industrial-led demand. The company issued higher third-quarter revenue and earnings guidance, reaffirmed its dividend, and continues its share repurchase program. This performance reinforces the bull case for Analog Devices, emphasizing its strength in industrial, AI data center, and automotive analog chips despite potential future market corrections.
